dateStatements.label: Manuscript production
dateStatements.value: Second quarter of the fifteenth century

entities.description: A paper codex of the second quarter of the fifteenth century containing an idiosyncratic South English Legendary compilation. The selected legends occur discontinuously from fol. 172r through the Germanus text ending in the current digital fol. 241av; numerous excluded legends intervene, and later selected lives share surfaces with Barlaam and Josaphat.
entities.label: Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. 779

physicalDescription.extent: ii + 312 leaves; foliation 1–306 omits inserted leaves now lettered after 110, 128, 141, 229, 241, and 252; former-binding matter and recovered fragments are stored with the manuscript
physicalDescription.format: Paper codex with a one-column layout, 41–60 written lines
notes: Written and extensively corrected, likely by one scribe in multiple phases, in cursive Anglicana.

The contemporary English white-leather-on-boards binding was replaced in 1998; its two wooden boards, thongs, straps, leather, parchment strengtheners, and recovered fragments are preserved in a phase box and fascicule.
Presented in 1611 by William Harwood, prebendary of Winchester.
Görlach states that the compiler modifies source-text orthography toward a Hampshire dialect; this is not evidence that every legend was composed there.

rights.basis: The anonymous medieval texts and physical witness substantially predate modern copyright.

access.accessMethod: No public complete page-image object was found. The exact catalog has no Digitised Content link, and the 10 October 2025 complete digitised-manuscript list passes directly from Egerton MS 2809 to Egerton MS 2818 without listing 2810.
notes: This negative finding is scoped to a complete public page-image facsimile; the physical manuscript remains extant and requestable.

access.accessMethod: Use the British Library online Imaging Services order form. Request the whole current manuscript—covers, spine, pastedowns, flyleaves, blanks, every recto and verso through fol. 188, edges and any associated fragments where offered—and specify colour photography. Identify the Corpus Christi work portion separately as ff. 176v–179v.
access.contact: British Library Imaging Services; licensing@bl.uk for reuse questions
access.format: Digital reference or reproduction photography; exact availability, handling, format, and whole-object quote subject to repository review
notes: The published 2025/26 rate card lists photographic TIFF at £27.70 for the first image and £7.25 per subsequent single-page image, while complex handling is £58.19 per image; a complete 188-folio manuscript request is a large order and should be treated as a custom quote rather than estimated mechanically.
The service says original manuscripts are photographed rather than scanned and that colour must be requested explicitly where a surrogate exists.

rights.basis: British Library Imaging Services says supplied material may carry rights and must not be copied or reproduced without written permission; order-specific terms would govern any delivered whole-manuscript images.
notes: A paid research delivery must not be placed in the public repository unless the order terms and a written licence expressly permit that reuse.

entities.description: A fourteenth-century parchment South English Legendary manuscript with fifteenth-century additions. Its Feast of Corpus Christi at ff. 176v–179v is the only parallel witness indexed by DIMEV for any of the thirty-three CME00077 parts; Horstmann did not use it for the 1889 edition.
entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 2810

physicalDescription.format: Codex of English verse saints' lives
notes: The British Library catalog reports a large lacuna between ff. 87v and 88 and several fifteenth-century additions in different hands.
The Feast of Corpus Christi begins at fol. 176v; DIMEV gives its end as fol. 179v.

rights.basis: The anonymous medieval text and physical witness substantially predate modern copyright.
rights.component: Fourteenth-century Feast of Corpus Christi witness

next_steps: Review the witness census in every DIMEV record and its cited current bibliography.
notes: DIMEV 1.0 contradicts a literal sole-witness reading for Corpus Christi by listing Egerton 2810, ff. 176v–179v; its other thirty-two cited records each contain one indexed witness. The remaining question concerns updates in post-release scholarship.
openQuestions.question: For which of the thirty-three texts does current scholarship still support Horstmann's 1889 claim that Bodley 779 is the only manuscript?

next_steps: Ask the Bodleian cataloging team only if a formal foliation crosswalk becomes necessary; retain both current claims meanwhile.
openQuestions.question: Can the Bodleian provide a formal concordance between the catalog's Germanus locus fols. 240v–241v and current Digital Bodleian labels fol. 240v through fol. 241av?

assertion.notes: This is a parallel witness, not a source used by Horstmann's 1889 edition.

relations.subjectLabel: DIMEV 1.0 records for the thirty-three selected legends

reviewNotes: The two printed runs are modeled as separate edition installments because the volume contents lists them separately and unrelated matter intervenes.
Whole-source completeness and work coverage are kept separate: the manuscript cache is the complete 672-canvas provider project, while work_portion records thirty-three discontiguous selections.
The broad facsimile start/end links are navigation anchors only; every included legend retains its own folio and canvas range.
The official catalog's date and Hampshire dialect statement describe the physical compilation and its scribe-compiler, not a single composition event for all selected legends.

The public manuscript photographs are CC BY-NC 4.0 and cannot be used in the repository's commercial print-on-demand pathway without separate permission, even though the medieval texts themselves are public domain.

DIMEV 1.0 exposes one parallel witness outside Bodley 779: Corpus Christi also survives in Egerton 2810. The current British Library catalog exposes no images, and the complete October 2025 digitised list omits that shelfmark, so the whole-object route requests all of Egerton 2810 and maps ff. 176v–179v separately.

lineage.summary: CME00077 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan encoding that joins Carl Horstmann's two 1889 Archiv installments, printed on pp. 307–353 and 369–422. Horstmann selected thirty-three anonymous Middle English verse legends from Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. 779 rather than printing that manuscript's complete South English Legendary compilation. Bodley 779 is the edition's direct source and Horstmann's claimed sole witness for the group or versions; DIMEV 1.0 nevertheless indexes a parallel Feast of Corpus Christi witness in British Library Egerton MS 2810, ff. 176v–179v. A complete 532-page journal volume, the pinned official Bodley catalog TEI, the complete DIMEV 1.0 data release, Kanno's complete 2016 thesis, and Digital Bodleian's complete 672-canvas manuscript project are cached locally. The full-codex image bundle retains boards, endleaves, every published manuscript leaf, parchment strengtheners, and detached medieval-binding leather. Its work_portion maps all thirty-three selected legends separately, including shared writing zones: Remigius begins partway down fol. 229av beside Barlaam and Josaphat, and Germanus ends partway down fol. 241av where Aethelwold begins. Egerton 2810 has no public complete facsimile in the current British Library record or October 2025 digitised-manuscript list; the manifest records a whole-object Imaging Services order route.

catalogSummary.author: Anonymous
catalogSummary.date: Selected manuscript forms first attested in the second quarter of the fifteenth century (1425–1450); constituent composition dates vary and remain unresolved here
catalogSummary.region: North Hampshire, England, for Bodley 779 compiler orthography; the places of composition of the selected legends are not established
catalogSummary.title: Younger Supplementary Legends from MS Bodley 779 (Horstmann selection)

catalogingSubject.label: Younger Supplementary Legends from MS Bodley 779 (Horstmann selection)
catalogingSubject.scope_note: The cataloged subject is Carl Horstmann's exact selection of thirty-three anonymous Middle English verse legends from Bodleian Library MS. Bodl. 779, printed in two 1889 journal installments and joined by the CME encoding. It is not the complete South English Legendary as copied in Bodley 779 and not one stable authorial work. The selection omits intervening manuscript items; several later selected legends occupy writing zones alongside Barlaam and Josaphat.

contentParts.label: Feast of Corpus Christi
notes: DIMEV 5329 indexes both Bodley 779 and British Library Egerton MS 2810, ff. 176v–179v; Horstmann's sole-witness claim therefore does not hold for this individual legend.
contentParts.summary: Verse legend and doctrinal account of the Corpus Christi feast.

contentParts.label: Life of St Francis
notes: DIMEV 5517; the manuscript ending is imperfect and the remainder of fol. 183r is blank.
contentParts.summary: Verse life of Saint Francis of Assisi.

contentParts.label: Life of St Faith
notes: DIMEV 2328.
contentParts.summary: Verse life and martyrdom of Saint Faith.

contentParts.label: Life of St Nycasie
notes: DIMEV 236.
contentParts.summary: Verse life and martyrdom of Saint Nicasius.

contentParts.label: Life of Pope Calixtus
notes: DIMEV 2608.
contentParts.summary: Verse papal life of Calixtus I.

contentParts.label: Life of St Winifred
notes: DIMEV 6698.
contentParts.summary: Verse life and martyrdom of the Welsh virgin Saint Winifred.

contentParts.label: Life of St Hilarion
notes: DIMEV 1500.
contentParts.summary: Verse life of the hermit Saint Hilarion.

contentParts.label: Lives of Saints Chrysanthus and Daria
notes: DIMEV 4385.
contentParts.summary: Paired verse martyr legend of Chrysanthus and Daria.

contentParts.label: Lives of Saints Crispin and Crispinian
notes: DIMEV 3574.
contentParts.summary: Paired verse martyr legend of Crispin and Crispinian.

contentParts.label: Life of St Evarist
notes: DIMEV 1209.
contentParts.summary: Verse papal life of Evaristus.

contentParts.label: Life of St Firmin
notes: DIMEV 4596.
contentParts.summary: Verse life of the bishop Saint Firmin.

contentParts.label: Life of St Vital
notes: DIMEV 6146.
contentParts.summary: Verse martyr legend of Saint Vitalis.

contentParts.label: Life of St Oswin
notes: DIMEV 2580.
contentParts.summary: Verse life and martyrdom of the Northumbrian king Saint Oswin.

contentParts.label: Lives of Saints John and Paul
notes: DIMEV 2954.
contentParts.summary: Paired verse martyr legend of the Roman brothers John and Paul.

contentParts.label: Life of Pope Leo II
notes: DIMEV 5728.
contentParts.summary: Short verse papal life of Leo II.

contentParts.label: Life of St Marius and His Family
notes: DIMEV 3408.
contentParts.summary: Verse martyr legend of Marius, Martha, Audifax, and Abachum.

contentParts.label: Life of St Illurin (Silverius)
notes: DIMEV 2437.
contentParts.summary: Verse papal martyr legend identified by the catalog with Silverius.

contentParts.label: Life of St Paulin (or Paul) the Hermit
notes: DIMEV 4257; the catalog reports the final ten manuscript lines on fol. 227r as crossed out.
contentParts.summary: Verse eremitic life of Paulin or Paul the Hermit.

contentParts.label: Life of St Sylvester
notes: DIMEV 555; Barlaam and Josaphat begins on fol. 228v, so the works overlap spatially on later surfaces.
contentParts.summary: Verse papal life of Sylvester I.

contentParts.label: Life of St Remigius
notes: DIMEV 5333; the catalog's end locus is inferred. Visual inspection places the opening partway down Digital Bodleian's fol. 229av canvas alongside Barlaam and Josaphat.
contentParts.summary: Verse episcopal life of Saint Remigius.

contentParts.label: Life of Pope Anicetus
notes: DIMEV 4541.
contentParts.summary: Short verse papal life of Anicetus.

contentParts.label: Life of Pope Soter
notes: DIMEV 5010; an XML milestone reads 182 where the manuscript and catalog support fol. 232.
contentParts.summary: Short verse papal life of Soter.

contentParts.label: Life of Pope Caius
notes: DIMEV 1499.
contentParts.summary: Short verse papal life of Caius.

contentParts.label: Life of St Emerentiana
notes: DIMEV 4533.
contentParts.summary: Short verse virgin-martyr legend of Emerentiana.

contentParts.label: Life of Pope Hyginus
notes: DIMEV 4616.
contentParts.summary: Short verse papal life of Hyginus.

contentParts.label: Life of Pope Miltiades
notes: DIMEV 3463.
contentParts.summary: Short verse papal life of Miltiades.

contentParts.label: Life of Pope Damasus
notes: DIMEV 4580.
contentParts.summary: Short verse papal life of Damasus I.

contentParts.label: Life of St Saturninus
notes: DIMEV 147.
contentParts.summary: Verse martyr legend of Saint Saturninus.

contentParts.label: Life of Pope Innocent I
notes: DIMEV 5336.

contentParts.label: Life of St Germanus
notes: DIMEV 4604; visual inspection shows Germanus begins at the top of canvas 496 (fol. 240v) and ends in the upper portion of canvas 498 (provider label fol. 241av), where the Life of St Aethelwold begins below.
contentParts.summary: Verse episcopal life and miracle narrative of Saint Germanus of Auxerre.

assertion.notes: Bodley 779 is the sole witness claimed for this selected group or these versions; DIMEV records the individual texts separately.

dateStatements.display: Second quarter of the fifteenth century (1425–1450)
notes: The statement applies to the exact selected manuscript forms, not necessarily to every underlying legend tradition.
The selection appeared in two installments in the same journal volume.

extent.description: Thirty-three DIV2 legend units; 5,182 XML L elements in 117 LG groups. The groups are encoding structures and are not asserted as canonical stanza counts.

formStatements.description: All thirty-three selected legends are represented as verse; the XML contains 5,182 L elements grouped in 117 LG elements.

notes: Horstmann's claim that Bodley 779 is the only manuscript is preserved as an edition-era claim about the selected group or versions; the thirty-three DIMEV records remain the safer unit for individual witness censuses.
DIMEV 1.0 gives a second Corpus Christi witness in British Library Egerton MS 2810, ff. 176v–179v; each of the other thirty-two selected records has one indexed witness in that release.
The preferred English title is supplied for discoverability and explicitly retains the edited-selection boundary.
The XML milestone in the Soter legend reading 182 is treated as a source/encoding typo because the official catalog and physical sequence support fol. 232.

next_steps: Review DIMEV record bibliographies and current scholarship for each constituent text.
notes: Do not convert the manuscript compiler's Hampshire orthography into a place-of-composition claim.
openQuestions.question: Which individual selected versions can be dated or localized more narrowly than their second-quarter-fifteenth-century witness?

next_steps: Ask the Bodleian cataloging team only if a formal foliation crosswalk becomes necessary; retain both current claims meanwhile.
openQuestions.question: Can the Bodleian supply a formal concordance between the catalog's Germanus locus fols. 240v–241v and the current Digital Bodleian labels fol. 240v through fol. 241av?

assertion.notes: Görlach localizes the compiler's orthographic modification of source texts; this does not prove the selected legends were composed there.

notes: Kept separate from place of composition and from physical manuscript production.

responsibilities.agentName: Anonymous authors of the selected legends

scope.description: Compilation and arrangement in the sole manuscript witness, not authorship of every constituent legend

summaries.value: An edited selection of thirty-three Middle English verse legends from the idiosyncratic fifteenth-century South English Legendary compilation in Bodley 779. The sequence opens with the Feast of Corpus Christi and includes lives of friars, hermits, bishops, martyrs, paired saints, English saints, and an especially dense group of popes. Horstmann presented these manuscript-specific additions or versions in two 1889 installments; the selection preserves neither the manuscript's full contents nor its continuous codex order.

Thirty-three verse legends totaling 5,182 encoded lines. Twelve appear on printed pp. 307–353 and twenty-one on pp. 369–422. The omitted printed pp. 354–368 are unrelated journal matter, and excluded manuscript legends intervene between many selected items.

assertion.notes: An English supplied title that preserves the edition's manuscript and selection boundary.

titles.value: Younger Supplementary Legends from MS Bodley 779 (Horstmann selection)
Des Ms. Bodl. 779 jüngere Zusatzlegenden zur südlichen Legendensammlung

The sequence opens with the Feast of Corpus Christi and includes lives of friars, hermits, bishops, martyrs, paired saints, English saints, and an especially dense group of popes. Horstmann presented these manuscript-specific additions or versions in two 1889 installments; the selection preserves neither the manuscript's full contents nor its continuous codex order.
